host: ubu-2 RTX 5070 sm_120 driver 580 (48 SMs, 0% util / 5 MiB at every fire)
toolchain: nvcc/ptxas 12.0 -arch=sm_90a (sm_120 via compute_90 PTX + driver JIT)
kernel: fa_dsweep — R10 multi-warp BM=64 + cp.async K/V double-buffer, parameterized over head_dim D; DTILES=D/16
baseline: cuBLAS-TC FP16 3-launch stack (GemmStridedBatchedEx x2 TENSOR_OP + OPTIMIZED block-per-row parallel softmax). NOTE: an earlier NAIVE one-thread-per-row softmax was a 13x-slower STRAWMAN (softmax alone 2.15 ms @ N=4096) — REJECTED; the fair optimized softmax matches R10's recorded baseline ballpark (0.176 vs R10 0.189 ms @ N=4096 d=64).

ptxas.smem_cap_check: max 38912 B @ d=256 << 49152 B per-block cap PASS; 0 spill at every d

numeric_gate.metric: per-row-scaled rel <= 1e-2 vs f64 CPU ref + NaN/Inf hard-fail (naninf==0)
numeric_gate.note: ALL 9 (N,d) shapes numeric PASS; d=64 reproduces R10's numbers exactly (faithful generalization)

timed_median_ms._method: cuEvent 20 warmup + 200 timed median, GPU uncontended (0% at start)

best_ratio.meaning: 22.5% ABOVE cuBLAS-TC stack (fused SLOWER); never crosses 1.0x

d_trend_verdict: FALSIFIED IN THE WRONG DIRECTION. Pre-registered hypothesis: d>=128 raises fused arithmetic intensity -> CROSS below 1.0x. OBSERVED: ratio WORSENS monotonically with d (d64 ~1.23x -> d128 ~1.75x -> d256 ~2.22x). cuBLAS's two big GEMMs are dominated by the d-INDEPENDENT N*N S HBM round-trip, so cuBLAS barely slows as d grows (0.176->0.233->0.354 ms @ N=4096). The fused per-warp serial d-tile loop (DTILES mma + scale_o + repack on the critical dependency chain) scales LINEARLY with d and degrades (0.217->0.409->0.786 ms). The d-lever moves the wrong way.
verdict: ORANGE (closed-negative on the d-lever). Numeric PASS all 9 shapes; best ratio 1.225x @ d=64 N=2048 NEVER crosses 1.0x; the d-trend DIVERGES from a crossover. The d-lever is RULED OUT — raising head_dim does NOT close the fused-vs-cuBLAS gap, it widens it. Best-d for the compute-pipeline probe (B) = d=64 (closest to cuBLAS).
